data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 96,000 USDT with a Narrowed 0.32% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 24, 2025, 09:28 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 96,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 96,000.007813 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.32% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Bybit CEO: We have returned to 1:1 rigid redemption and will soon launch a website to track the flow of stolen funds
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou said in a community AMA that he has obtained the latest asset proof audit report, which will be updated on the official website soon. Bybit has returned to 1:1 rigid redemption, and Bybit's capital inflow in the past 24 hours ranks first among global CEXs. Bybit will launch a website on the flow of stolen funds, and will simultaneously update data such as the progress of recovery. Users who provide effective clues will receive a bounty. The new section of the Bybit New Coin Announcement column regularly publishes the performance and financial reports of the online projects, covering 5 dimensions: core financial data, token indicators, on-chain activities and network health, governance and community, as well as risk disclosure and future roadmaps.

data.title: In the past 2 hours, 7 newly created wallets deposited 116.62 million BAN to CEX, and the sale will lose $5.63 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to The Data Nerd's monitoring, in the past 2 hours, 7 newly-created wallets deposited 116.62 million BAN into Gate, with a value of approximately US$10.44 million, accounting for 11.66% of the total supply. These tokens were purchased two days ago at an average entry price of US$0.133. If all are sold at the current price, a loss of approximately US$5.63 million will occur.

data.title: US Bitcoin ETFs lose $1.14B in two weeks amid US-China trade tensions
data.description:
US Bitcoin ETFs saw $1.14 billion in outflows over two weeks, the largest since their launch, as trade tensions and monetary policy concerns weighed on investor sentiment.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-etfs-hit-1-14-b-outflows-2-weeks-us-china-trade-tensions?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Stablecoins Emerge As New Payment Method Amid Challenges
data.description:
According to Foresight News, DL News reports that Chris Colson, a payments researcher at the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta, has highlighted the growing adoption of stablecoins as a new payment method, despite facing several challenges. Colson noted that some retailers have already begun accepting stablecoin payments, and the market size for stablecoins has surpassed $230 billion, which is comparable to the annual GDP of Greece or New Zealand.

Colson emphasized that for stablecoins to achieve widespread adoption, they must gain acceptance from banks, credit card companies, and regulatory bodies. He pointed out that since stablecoin transactions are based on blockchain technology, they lack the ability to reverse transactions as traditional banking payments do, posing a challenge that needs to be addressed during their promotion. He also mentioned that similar to Apple Pay, which took a decade to gain widespread user acceptance, stablecoins may require time to be broadly embraced by users.

data.title: Infini founder: Funds are ready and will respond to any withdrawal requests in the middle
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Infini founder Christian said in a post: "70% of the stolen $50 million belonged to big friends I know. I have communicated with them one by one and I will personally bear the possible losses and settle privately. The remaining funds will be reinvested in the infini vault before next Monday, and everything will remain the same. The funds have been prepared and will respond to any withdrawal requests in the middle, so please rest assured. It will take some time to upgrade and restart the business, and everything will be carried out under the premise of ensuring the absolute safety of funds."
